tagline: Convert partitioned Delta Lake tables to liquid clustering with minimal downtime.
what_it_does: This feature allows users to convert existing partitioned Delta Lake tables to liquid clustering using the `ALTER TABLE ... REPLACE PARTITIONED BY WITH CLUSTER BY` command. It minimizes reader and writer downtime during the conversion process and supports both external and managed tables. Liquid clustering optimizes query performance by automatically organizing data based on clustering keys, making it easier to adapt to changing analytic needs.
why_it_matters: This update enhances data management efficiency and query performance, particularly for organizations dealing with high cardinality columns and heavy data skew. By simplifying the data layout and reducing maintenance efforts, businesses can achieve better performance and lower operational costs, ultimately improving productivity and responsiveness to changing data access patterns.
plain_summary: This feature allows users to convert existing partitioned Delta Lake tables to liquid clustering using the ALTER TABLE ... REPLACE PARTITIONED BY WITH CLUSTER BY command.

capabilities.title: Minimized Downtime
capabilities.desc: Conversion minimizes reader and writer downtime during the transition.

capabilities.title: Flexible Clustering Keys
capabilities.desc: Allows redefining clustering keys without rewriting existing data.

capabilities.title: Performance Optimization
capabilities.desc: Improves query performance by automatically organizing data based on clustering keys.

capabilities.title: Support for Streaming
capabilities.desc: Liquid clustering applies to both streaming tables and materialized views.

prerequisites: Databricks Runtime 18.1 or above for conversion.
Existing Delta Lake table must be partitioned.
use_cases: Optimizing queries on high cardinality columns.
Managing tables with heavy data skew.
Supporting fast-growing tables with concurrent write requirements.
Adapting to varied or changing access patterns.
limitations: Streaming tables and materialized views in Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines are not supported.
Tables using Delta Sharing with partition filtering are not supported.

steps.title: Run Conversion Command
steps.desc: Use `ALTER TABLE <table_name> REPLACE PARTITIONED BY WITH CLUSTER BY (<clustering_columns>)`.

steps.title: Optimize Table
steps.desc: Run `OPTIMIZE <table_name>` to apply clustering after conversion.

steps.title: Verify Conversion
steps.desc: Run `DESCRIBE EXTENDED <table_name>` to check new clustering columns.

steps.title: Handle Concurrent Workloads
steps.desc: Ensure Databricks Runtime 15.4 LTS or above for concurrent writes during conversion.

steps.title: Rollback if Necessary
steps.desc: Use `RESTORE` to revert to the previous version if needed.

key_takeaway: The ability to convert partitioned tables to liquid clustering enhances performance and simplifies data management.
